overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California Business & Professions Code generally voids non-compete agreements. A CA-based subfranchisor may not be bound by the 24-month post-term restriction

overrides.override_summary: Florida choice of law may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA courts may apply CA law instead of FL law, which could change multiple contract interpretations

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer may be void under CA Corporations Code §31512 and Business & Professions Code §20010
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not be required to waive Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act claims at renewal

overrides.override_summary: Binding arbitration in FL venue notice — CA franchisees encouraged to consult counsel on applicability of CA law and Federal Arbitration Act
overrides.why_it_matters: FL arbitration venue may be challengeable under CA law

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: 11 USC §365 may protect franchisee's rights in bankruptcy

overrides.override_summary: Maximum price agreements viewed as per se violations of Cartwright Act by CA AG
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor will not interpret agreement as permitting or requiring maximum price limits in CA

overrides.override_summary: Choice of forum/venue provision deleted entirely. §4 of IL Franchise Disclosure Act voids out-of-state venue clauses for litigation (arbitration in non-IL venue may still be permitted)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in FL courts

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the agreements (not Florida)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise protections apply in full

overrides.override_summary: §41 of IL Franchise Disclosure Act voids any provision purporting to waive compliance with IL law
overrides.why_it_matters: General release and other waiver provisions unenforceable to extent they waive IL franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees preserve right to assert MD franchise law claims even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy protection applies

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD Franchise Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: MD has a specific 3-year SOL for franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: MD regulation states requiring franchisee to waive right to file lawsuit in MD is unfair/deceptive practice. Enforceability of FL arbitration requirement disputed under Federal Arbitration Act
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees may challenge FL arbitration venue

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause. Good cause requires failure to comply + written notice + 30 days to cure
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law provides stronger termination protections than the agreement alone

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to renew without fair compensation (repurchase or FMV) for inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures — unless term ≥5 years or 6-month advance non-renewal notice given
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees may be entitled to FMV compensation on non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan. However, franchisor states it will seek to enforce arbitration provisions and believes MI law is unconstitutional on this point
overrides.why_it_matters: Arbitration venue is disputed — franchisor explicitly challenges MI law

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination + 60 days to cure (vs 30 days in agreement). 180 days notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees get substantially longer notice periods

overrides.override_summary: MN law may prohibit requiring waiver of jury trial, consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Several agreement provisions may be unenforceable in MN

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under MN Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: NY GBL Article 33 rights preserved despite general release requirements. §687(4) and (5) non-waiver provisions apply
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law claims cannot be waived

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate agreement on any grounds available by law (replacing 'not applicable' in Item 17)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY explicitly grants franchisee termination right not present in base agreement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot cancel without reasonable cause per VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: VA provides additional termination protections; unreasonable termination grounds may be unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails over any inconsistent FDD or agreement provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law is the ceiling — agreement cannot provide less protection
